steps.title: Find the key signal in the passage
steps.content: The word "but" signals a reversal: a damaged fossil should logically give less information, yet the broken area on this trilobite fossil gave Schoenemann something valuable. The blank needs a verb meaning the fossil yielded or gave fresh insight.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — resolved: means determined or solved a problem. A fossil cannot actively resolve anything — it is an inanimate object that provides information. ✗
  • Choice B — adjusted: means modified or changed. The fossil did not change the insight; it provided it. ✗
  • Choice C — offered: means provided or made available. The broken area offered, or yielded, fresh insight into the trilobite's eye structure — a natural fit. ✓
  • Choice D — directed: means managed or aimed. The fossil did not direct insight; it made insight available. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
